title: Treatment outcome of gemcitabine and cisplatin induction followed by concurrent chemoradiotherapy for stage III-IVA nasopharyngeal carcinoma: A retrospective study
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"58guv","text":"BackgroundCombining induction
chemotherapy with concurrent chemoradiotherapy is becoming increasingly popular
for treating stage III-IVA nasopharyngeal cancer (NPC), especially in patients
with Epstein-Barr virus (EBV)-associated disorders. The objective of this study
is to assess the efficacy of this therapy regimen in terms of treatment
response and disease control.","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33e8j","text":"Method","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c9hvs","text":"This
retrospective descriptive study analyzed 81 patients with stage III-IVA
nasopharyngeal cancer (excluding T3N0M0) who underwent gemcitabine and
cisplatin induction chemotherapy, followed by concurrent chemoradiotherapy at
the Vietnam National Cancer Hospital. Data for the study were collected between
03/06/2021 and 03/06/2024. The study also involved a longitudinal follow-up of
the patients. The primary endpoint was disease-free survival (DFS), with
secondary outcomes assessing factors influencing DFS and treatment-related
toxicity.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"csvve","text":"Results","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d6dmp","text":"During the initial
3-month period, 76 out of 81 patients achieved a complete response (CR),
whereas 5 out of 81 patients achieved a partial response. The duration of the
follow-up period was 18.7 ± 5.3 months, and the rate of 2-year disease-free
survival (DFS) was 77.6%. The disease-free survival (DFS) was influenced by
patient-related variables including age group, N stage, disease stage, and
treatment interruptions. The toxicity observed in grade 3 were neutropenia
(17.3%) and mucositis (32.1%), whereas grade 4 toxic effect was characterized
by nausea (2.4%). 2.6% of patients experienced Grade I and II delayed toxic
effects, as well as Grade III anorexia.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"52m82","text":"Conclusion","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjcd","text":"The combination of Gemcitabine and Cisplatin as an
initial treatment, followed by chemoradiotherapy, demonstrated a significant
rate of positive response, effectively controlling the disease with tolerable
consequences. Additional investigation is required to ascertain the long-term
effectiveness and delayed adverse effects.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bi6gl","text":"Patient Selection Criteria: Ensure that only patients meeting the inclusion criteria are enrolled. Obtain informed consent after explaining all risks, benefits, and potential outcomes of the treatment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fv4uk","text":"Chemotherapy Administration: Adhere to the prescribed dosages for gemcitabine and cisplatin. Monitor patients closely for toxicity or adverse reactions during each cycle.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1v9p4","text":"Radiation Safety: During concurrent chemoradiotherapy, ensure all radiation safety protocols are followed to prevent unnec","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cgj52","text":"Medical History: Review the patient’s medical history to assess suitability for chemotherapy and radiation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aq66s","text":"Physical Examination: Perform a thorough examination to evaluate overall health status.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mpne","text":"Laboratory Tests: Conduct necessary tests (e.g., blood work, organ function tests) to ensure the patient is fit for treatment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"edneb","text":"Toxicity Risk: The combination of gemcitabine and cisplatin carries a risk of severe toxicity. Immediate medical attention is required if patients exhibit signs of serious toxicity, such as renal impairment or severe myelosuppression.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7qous","text":"Radiation Exposure: Ensure strict adherence to radiation protocols to prevent overexposure, which can lead to long-term health risks for b","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3oof7","text":"Adverse Effects: Closely monitor patients for severe side effects, including nausea, vomiting, neutropenia, or neuropathy. If side effects become intolerable, treatment may need to be paused or adjusted.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6cmel","text":"Early Termination: If a patient develops severe contraindications or life-threatening side effects, early termination of the treatment protocol is advised to prevent further harm.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"19hi7","text":"Method","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqoqj","text":"This study is a retrospective descriptive investigation that involves longitudinal follow-up from 03/06/2021 to 03/06/2024. It includes a total of 81 patients who were treated at the Vietnam National Cancer Hospital. The eligibility requirements are as follows: a) confirmation of NPC through pathological diagnosis, b) classification as stage III or IVA according to the AJCC 8th edition, c) undergoing treatment with Gemcitabine and Cisplatin induction chemotherapy followed by concurrent chemoradiotherapy, d) having an ECOG performance status score of 0-1, and e) possessing accessible patient data. The exclusion criteria include a) T3N0M0 stage, b) secondary cancers, c) chronic disorders such as heart failure or renal failure, d) prior treatment of the
nasopharynx and/or neck region with radiotherapy, chemotherapy, or surgery, and e) pregnancy. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e4kdr","text":"All patients at Vietnam National Cancer Hospital received the same treatment protocol. We documented the patient's attributes and their responses to therapy, and we monitored the occurrence of relapse, advancement, metastasis, the period without disease (disease-free survival), and harmful effects through
follow-up phone calls and subsequent examinations. The data were accessed and analyzed by the authors for research purposes from July 1, 2024, to September 1, 2024.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4c74f","text":"Endpoints","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vf5c","text":"The primary endpoint is disease-free survival (DFS) that refers to the period following the completion of initial cancer therapy, during which the patient is alive and free from any indications or symptoms of the cancer. Secondary endpoints encompass variables influencing DFS and adverse consequences.","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b84p4","text":"Statistical analysis","type":"header-two","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"865ju","text":"Data
were analyzed using SPSS version 20.0, employing the following methods:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3m3fk","text":"     Descriptive
statistics: Mean, standard deviation, maximum, and minimum values.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ce5ba","text":"     Chi-square
(χ²) test: 95% confidence interval, p < 0.05.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e657i","text":"     Kaplan-Meier
method: Evaluating disease-free survival (DFS) duration, with group comparisons
using the Log-rank test (p < 0.05).
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Son Nguyen Viet
